title: Frozen White Chocolate Mousse Cake with Raspberry Sauce
ingredients: 1 cup vanilla or chocolate wafer crumbs
6 ounces plus 2 ounces white chocolate, chopped
2 cups fresh or frozen unsweetened raspberries
instructions: Combine wafer crumbs and melted butter without using a food processor.
Press the mixture evenly into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan.
Refrigerate the crust.
Melt 6 ounces of white chocolate in the top of a double boiler, stirring until smooth, then cool slightly.
Bring sugar and water to a boil in a small saucepan, stirring until sugar dissolves.
Continue boiling without stirring until a candy thermometer reads 238°F (soft-ball stage).
Beat egg whites and cream of tartar until soft peaks form.
Gradually beat the hot sugar syrup into the egg whites.
Continue beating until the mixture is stiff and glossy, about 3 minutes.
Fold the warm chocolate into the egg white mixture.
Refrigerate the chocolate mixture until cool but not set, about 5 minutes.
Whip the chilled cream, Grand Marnier, and vanilla extract in a large bowl until soft peaks form.
Gently fold the whipped cream into the chocolate mixture.
Fold the remaining 2 ounces of white chocolate into the mousse.
Pour the mousse into the prepared crust and smooth the top.
Sprinkle the pistachios over the mousse.
Freeze until firm, about 6 hours.
Cover with plastic wrap and return to freezer.
Purée the raspberries in a blender.
Strain the purée through a fine sieve to remove seeds.
Stir in sugar and Grand Marnier.
Refrigerate the raspberry sauce at least 1 hour.
Remove the mousse cake from freezer just before serving.
Spoon sauce onto individual plates and place a slice of cake on each.
Top with fresh raspberries and white chocolate shavings.
notes: Do not use a food processor when combining crust ingredients.
Both mousse cake and raspberry sauce can be prepared up to 3 days before serving.
The mousse cake should be covered with plastic wrap after freezing to prevent freezer burn.
